title: karpatiosorbus houstoniae
intro: Karpatiosorbus houstoniae, also known as Houston's whitebeam or Houston's rowan, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a small deciduous tree or shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Karpatiosorbus houstoniae include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities, making it a valuable herbal preparation in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts.
